ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, APPLICATION FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANTS KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. AND K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC. IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF AGAINST DEFENDANTS KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. AND K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC. IN THE AMOUNTS AS FOLLOWS: AS TO KELLY MOORE PAINT CO., INC: $2,000,000.00; AND AS TO K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC.: $2,000,000.00. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc. And K-M Industries Holding Co., Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-26-2025 Text Ruling

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2023 Set For Trial On Nov-27-23 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Nov-22-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Adjudication is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of withdrawal filed by Defendant on October 6, 2023. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Oct-09-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Aug-10-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Robert J. Bugatto of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Foster Wheeler LLC
Saleem Belbahri of FOLEY & MANSFIELD LLP (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Mariam Pambukyan of WFBM, LLP (714-634-2522), appearing for defendant General Electric Company and Paramount Global

RULING:
Not contested, not argued, and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date is November 27, 2023 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is December 8, 2023. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is November 22, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is November 10, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is November 24, 2023. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 7, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
Off Calendar Aug-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than August 24, 2023, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than August 24, 2023, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on October 12, 2023 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than October 13, 2023. Responses to the demand are due no later than October 27, 2023. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Pang Ly via electronic mail by November 17, 2023 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Wednesday, November 22, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: D. Au/J.Alameda. Not reported. (503/SKF)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-27-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Not contested, no appearances, and tentative ruling adopted: Specially Appearing Defendant Weyerhaeuser Company's Motion to Quash Service of Summons for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of withdrawal filed by moving party on April 24, 2023. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)
